target: WIN-048 detail_extra — annotate dome self-correction of confidence level (>99.9% → 2-3σ)
replacement_html:
Update (2026-04-10): The dome author revised the WIN-048 confidence claim on 2026-03-23, acknowledging that the original ">99.9% confirmed by two missions" framing overstated the Planck full-mission result. The dome site now states verbatim: "Planck full-mission significance is 2–3σ — not the >99.9% originally reported by WMAP. The claim ‘confirmed at >99.9% by two missions’ overstated the Planck result. Confidence level corrected accordingly." The entry further notes that "the 2–3σ anomaly continues to favour ECM structurally while remaining below the threshold for definitive confirmation" and classifies the result as CONSISTENT rather than confirmed, with a caveat that galactic masking artefacts account for ~20% of simulated alignments.

Kernel of truth: this is a responsible self-correction and we acknowledge it. The author voluntarily demoted a headline confidence number after the Planck reanalysis rather than hide behind the older WMAP figure, which is more intellectual honesty than most advocacy sites manage. On that narrow process point, the dome author gets it right.

But the correction does not rescue WIN-048. Three reasons our verdict stands:
  1. The false dilemma survives unchanged. Whether the Axis of Evil is a 6σ or 2σ anomaly, "ΛCDM struggles with this" is not evidence for a dome. The dome’s own WIN-048 entry still frames this as a "ΛCDM Counter-Prediction" — a single-bit negative signal about a competing model cannot distinguish a flat dome from dozens of alternative cosmologies that also accommodate CMB anomalies.
  2. 2–3σ lands exactly where our rebuttal already put it. We cited Planck 2015 Results XXIII (alignment p-values 0.5%–5% depending on estimator and mask) and Planck 2018 isotropy results. The dome’s new self-assessment of 2–3σ (p ≈ 0.3%–5%) sits inside the same range, so the corrected dome claim is now numerically compatible with the mainstream Planck consensus — the "anomaly" the dome still leans on is the same equivocal low-multipole oddity Planck itself treats as look-elsewhere sensitive.
  3. The WIN-034 self-contradiction is untouched. Downgrading confidence in the CMB alignment does nothing to resolve the geometric problem we raised: a cast copper/bronze firmament (WIN-034) has a ~0.2 μm skin depth at 160 GHz and would be completely opaque to the CMB. Either the firmament is microwave-transparent (contradicting WIN-034) or there is no CMB to have an Axis of Evil in (contradicting WIN-048’s premise). Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper.
Net effect on verdict: "Misleading" still applies. The weakened claim is less misleading than the original, and we credit the author for the correction, but the structural problems (false dilemma, self-contradiction with WIN-034, no dome mechanism for CMB generation in flat geometry) are independent of the σ-value and remain fatal.
dome_verbatim_quotes: Planck full-mission significance is 2–3σ — not the >99.9% originally reported by WMAP. The claim “confirmed at >99.9% by two missions” overstated the Planck result. Confidence level corrected accordingly.
The 2–3σ anomaly continues to favour ECM structurally while remaining below the threshold for definitive confirmation.
holes_addressed: ISS-673 fairness concern: our review did not acknowledge the dome self-correction
Poller flag 2026-04-09: W048 confidence downgrade not reflected in our text
kernel_of_truth: The dome author voluntarily demoted the WIN-048 confidence claim after Planck full-mission reanalysis rather than continue citing the inflated WMAP figure. This is genuine intellectual honesty on a specific process point and we should name it as such before explaining why the underlying verdict is unchanged.
why_verdict_unchanged: False dilemma (LCDM counter-prediction is not dome evidence) is independent of statistical significance

Dome-corrected confidence (2-3 sigma) lands inside the same 0.5-5% p-value range our rebuttal already cited from Planck 2015/2018; corrected claim is now numerically compatible with the mainstream consensus
WIN-034 copper firmament opacity at 160 GHz (~0.2 micron skin depth) is a geometric contradiction untouched by any change in CMB alignment statistics

anticipated_objections.objection: You are piling on. The author already corrected the claim, why keep the Misleading verdict?
anticipated_objections.response: The Misleading verdict never rested on the inflated >99.9% figure alone. It rested on (a) the false-dilemma framing, (b) the copper-firmament self-contradiction with WIN-034, and (c) the absence of any dome mechanism for CMB generation. Lowering the significance number fixes none of those. We explicitly acknowledge the correction and credit the author for it in the update, which is the fair move.

anticipated_objections.objection: A 2-3 sigma anomaly is still an anomaly. Why dismiss it?
anticipated_objections.response: We do not dismiss it. We note the dome now assesses the same equivocal low-multipole oddity Planck 2018 itself characterises as look-elsewhere sensitive and mask-dependent. The dome and mainstream cosmology now agree on the size of the effect; the disagreement is purely about interpretation, and that is a theoretical-framing question a 2 sigma anomaly cannot settle.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should the verdict move from Misleading to something softer given the correction?
anticipated_objections.response: Arguable. We considered Not Demonstrated. We kept Misleading because the LCDM counter-prediction framing, which is the single largest reason the claim is Misleading, is a pure framing choice the author has not revised. If the dome retired the "counter-prediction" framing the verdict would be worth re-opening.

integration_instructions: Set wins.json entry 048 detail_extra to the replacement_html value. No other field changes. detail_claim, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, verdict (Misleading), and code_analysis tags remain unchanged.
notes: Fetched verbatim quotes from john09289.github.io/predictions/wins on 2026-04-10 via WebFetch. Dome revision dated 2026-03-23 per the same page. Considered but rejected a verdict move to Not Demonstrated (see third anticipated objection).
